diagrams.title: CAP Theorem Triangle
diagrams.description: Visual representation of CAP theorem showing the three properties and tradeoffs during network partitions
diagrams.purpose: Understanding fundamental tradeoff in distributed databases - must choose between Consistency or Availability when partition occurs.

diagrams.title: NoSQL Database Types
diagrams.description: Four main types of NoSQL databases with examples and data models
elements: Graph databases - Neo4j, Titan (nodes and edges)
diagrams.purpose: Categorizing and understanding different NoSQL database architectures and their use cases.

diagrams.title: ACID vs BASE Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of ACID (RDBMS) and BASE (NoSQL) properties
elements: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent (BASE)
RDBMS use cases vs NoSQL use cases
diagrams.purpose: Understanding transaction models and why NoSQL chooses different approach for scalability.

diagrams.title: Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling
diagrams.description: Comparison of scaling approaches showing how NoSQL enables horizontal scaling
elements: Vertical scaling diagram: Single server getting more CPU/RAM
Horizontal scaling diagram: Multiple servers connected in cluster
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why NoSQL databases prefer horizontal scaling and how it enables massive scalability.

diagrams.title: Eventual Consistency Timeline
diagrams.description: Visual timeline showing how data propagates across replicas in eventually consistent systems
diagrams.purpose: Understanding eventual consistency - data becomes consistent across all nodes over time without blocking.
